checkpoint: uncommitted structural exception reduction with the active feature still in progress
scope: Owner-preserving test modularization, taxonomy compaction, and exact no-growth exception reduction
observed_implementation: Fourteen Rust test owners were split into focused child or sibling modules without changing production behavior.
Ten frontend and development-runtime test owners were split or deduplicated while retaining their existing test coverage.
Six script-test owners were split below the canonical hard ceilings after the complete script-test tree moved under owner-mirrored directories.
The job-scoring synonym taxonomy was compacted without changing its parsed data.
Seventeen retained script-test exceptions moved to owner-mirrored paths without increasing their exact line or byte ceilings.
The repository exact no-growth exception count fell by 31 net exceptions, from 109 to 78.
The security sensor CODEOWNERS boundary now points to the owner-mirrored security test path.

verification.result: All 390 notification tests and crate documentation tests passed.
Every changed owner stayed below its canonical ceiling or retained exact no-growth ceiling.
All 41 focused security sensor tests, the security policy check, and the secret scan passed.

Repository contracts, security checks, 817 script tests, 2933 frontend tests, production and Storybook builds, the 10-test smoke journey, Rust formatting, Clippy with warnings denied, the full Rust workspace tests, documentation tests, and patch whitespace checks passed.

remaining_implementation: The remaining 78 structural exceptions still require prioritized reduction or justified retention.
The final whole-repository audit and active feature transition have not occurred.
